title: Application Metadata, Transactions, Events, and Orphans · Execution Trace: Database Object Ordering
summary: A database row says ready before S3 accepts bytes, an object exists without an owner row after rollback, and events race ahead of scan and authorization state. Predict the representation, storage, trust, cost, and recovery evidence before inspecting a model. Planned artifact: Add and verify the database object ordering increment in file metadata and object reconciliation state machine. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence d…
objectives: Choose database-first, object-first, or direct-upload ordering and expose each crash window, compensation, and user-visible state.
Persist idempotent intent, reservation, object identity, expected integrity, upload, quarantine, publish, failure, and expiry transitions.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: S3, CloudFront, and Go Predict Database Object Ordering · Theory Database Object Ordering
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory Go file-delivery team handles case 3935 during a private media release. Build deterministic Go evidence to expose object and database crash windows and require idempotent reconciliation; record cleanup, rollback, restoration, and residual-risk ownership; then introduce a duplicate event or ambiguous response and reconcile without duplicate effect. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. AWS SDK packages, networks, S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, databases, credentials, host state, race and leak checks, load, faults, restore, billing, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.
